title: Good to Great
author: Jim Collins
subline: Why some companies make the leap — and others don't.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <g transform="translate(160 1200)" fill="none">
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.5">
      <path d="M 0 380 L 200 376 L 400 360 L 600 350 L 800 340 L 1000 330 L 1200 320"/>
      <path d="M 0 420 L 200 415 L 400 410 L 600 400 L 800 395 L 1000 385 L 1200 380"/>
      <path d="M 0 460 L 200 460 L 400 455 L 600 450 L 800 450 L 1000 445 L 1200 440"/>
      <path d="M 0 500 L 200 495 L 400 490 L 600 485 L 800 480 L 1000 478 L 1200 470"/>
      <path d="M 0 540 L 200 530 L 400 520 L 600 510 L 800 500 L 1000 490 L 1200 480"/>
    </g>
    <g stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="square">
      <path d="M 0 350 L 400 348 L 500 340 L 550 330 L 600 310 L 650 280 L 700 240 L 750 180 L 800 100 L 900 40 L 1000 -20" stroke="#cccccc"/>
      <path d="M 600 310 L 650 280 L 700 240 L 750 180 L 800 100 L 900 40 L 1000 -20" stroke="#fc1c46"/>
    </g>
    <line x1="0" y1="600" x2="1280" y2="600" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <g font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">
      <text x="0" y="640">30 YR</text>
      <text x="1200" y="640" text-anchor="end">→</text>
    </g>
    <text x="-20" y="370" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">MARKET</text>
    <text x="-20" y="386" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">AVERAGE</text>
    <text x="700" y="160" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.18em">THE LEAP</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A 2D stock chart is the visual story of the book. The 'great' company line traces flat gray for 60% of its run, then climbs sharply — that climb is RED, the only chromatic move. Five flatter gray 'comparison' lines below. 'THE LEAP' label in red. 340px uppercase title sits at top. No decoration — the chart IS the argument.
